text: Yes, towards a decision on this at this special session, we regret that our delegation would not be in a position to seek to be proposal. Thank you, sir. Thank you, Argentina. France, please. Thank you, Mr President. Allow me to give you a good day. You are always elegant and understanding the way you are presented. We would like to express condolences to the delegation of Kenya. We support the position of Denmark. We are in favour, in principle, to the establishment of a subcommittee with the proviso that this should continue to expedite the work underway and future. That means that measures should be taken this week. It is in that framework that we could agree to this proposal. We would also recall our traditional attachment to the importance of Martellin-Wilism in the UN system that has certainly been the token of our commitment and effectiveness of our work, so that all countries are on an equal footing. And also to better structure the work flow while bearing in mind the need to ensure participation for all delegations. We would recall our active involvement in the funding of the multi-donor trust fund for Wiener's gases in order to allow for effective participation of all. Thank you, sir. Thank you, France. Senegal, please. Distinguished delegates, ladies and gentlemen. The delegation of Senegal would like to offer its deepest condolences to the delegation of Kenya. We would like to thank Chile and France for refreshments yesterday. The work plan for implementation of the Net Zero Framework has a number of work streams which are regulatory and financial in nature for the establishment of the fund and the monitoring mechanisms. The substantial work required which cannot just be done within the intercessional working group. And thus, the establishment of a permanent subcommittee as proposed by Egypt and the EU, whose sponsors of this document would provide a framework which could do with these work streams and ensure continuity and tangible outcomes within a timeline. The current working of the ISWG, often in English only with rather short term organization has made it difficult for countries speaking in different language including the Francophone West African countries and I am one of those that makes it difficult to express ourselves. And as a subcommittee is a regulatory body, a part of the IMO would comply with the requirements of material, legalism and better access to documents and greater participation. That would be in keeping with our principles of fairness here at IMO. We therefore fully support the establishment of this subcommittee and prepared to discuss its terms of reference and work procedures. Thank you very much, sir. Thank you, Senegal. United Kingdom, please. Thank you very much, chair. I would like to start by expressing the United Kingdom since their condolences to the delegation of Kenya on this day of morning. The UK recognizes the amount of work relating to greenhouse gases facing the organization in the coming years and the great importance of this work to our sector and our planet. We would like to thank Egypt at all for their proposal of a new GHG subcommittee. I would also like to thank the distinguished delegate of Morocco for presenting this proposal and for the hard work on this issue. The proposal raises some important considerations with regards to workload and multilegalism and we are open to discussing working arrangements. Our priority at the session is to ensure the net zero framework is adopted. We wonder if the committee has sufficient capacity to adequately consider the budgetary and practical implications of this proposal, but where there is a will that can perhaps be away. In particular, we believe we need to thoroughly consider and finalize a terms of reference of a new subcommittee. We believe the proposal, the proposed terms of reference is a mission 232 require considerable work among member states to, for example, ensure it is sufficiently aligned with a work plan, which we understand to be more holistic picture of the GHG work ahead for the organization. We would like to propose that the Secretary prepares a terms of reference to be the basis of the committee's consideration, taking account of the comments made by delegation during this discussion. Many thanks to all delegations for their views on this issue. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, United Kingdom. Cyprus, please. Thank you, Chair, and good morning. Firstly, we want to convey our condolences to the delegation of Kenya. We want to thank the Consponsors, Egypt, Kenya, Turkey and others, and we support in principle under a very simple condition that such an arrangement will facilitate our work and they will not create additional obstacles in the issues of the crucial issues of greenhouse gases and ends of the city. We do acknowledge the impact of the extra cost, especially as member of the Council of this organization. As described on information, contain on document ES2-3-4-3-4 by the Secretary General. Thank you. Thank you, Cyprus. To value, please. Thank you, Chair. To our delegation, we would also like to convey our condolences to the delegation of Kenya. Chair, to value fully understand the needs for efficient work and the attractiveness of a dedicated subcommittee on GHD to be able to deal with the enormous amount of work that will be required for the IAMO to deliver on its commitments. To value is open to consider this proposal, but is concerned about the support available for seats and the disease to attend sessions of the subcommittee. Would the voluntary or multi-donovan trust fund be extended to this? How would that fund be guaranteed to match the needs arising out of the establishment of such a committee? So, inclusivity, we dependant on voluntary donation, is this sustainable for the IAMO. Furthermore, as alluded by the Distinguished Delegates from Brazil yesterday, the parallel work stream that will result from the establishment of a dedicated subcommittee might make it very difficult for seats and the disease, small delegations to fully engage and participate to follow all of them. So, as such, Tvalu simply requests that the decision-making process, Joseph, is inclusive, transparent, just, and equitable. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Tvalu. Marisha, please. Thank you, Chair. Good morning to all delegates. Marisha wishes to thank all delegations who have entered in us with tea, coffee, lunches, and others. Also, our sincere condolences to the delegation of Kenya. Marisha also thank all contributors to the voluntary multi-donovan trust fund, which is helping delegations that Marisha's to participate in this meeting. Marisha supports in principle the setting up of the proposed subcommittee. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Marisha. One last couple, please. I want to thank Mr. President. I would also like to extend Monaco's condolences to the people and government of Kenya following the passing of Raíla Odinga Mayharest in peace. The principality of Monaco attaches particular importance to the reduction of GHG gas emissions from shipping. It's a major challenge with regard to climate change. We support the establishment of such a subcommittee, particularly given its effect on the effectiveness of our work. This is a matter we're particularly aware of, and that is the importance of effective multilingualism. It is not an option. It's an imperative. It's far too important for us to continue to go along with its insufficient implementation. Without multilingualism, we cannot expect effective implementation of our rules and our goals established in the IMO in order to reduce greenhouse gases. Thank you. Thank you, Monaco. Namibia, please. Thank you, Chair, and good morning to all. First, we wish to convey our most sincere condolences to the delegation of Kenya on the passing of former Prime Minister, Honra Bu-Raila Odinga. On the matter at hand, we thank the co-sponsors of the document and our discussion. Chair, reducing greenhouse gas emissions is one of humanity's greatest challenges. We agree with the co-sponsors and many previous speakers that the net zero framework carries a substantial workload, and that the standing structure can improve effectiveness and inclusivity, especially with full services, with full language services, and participation support for developing countries. With this in mind, Chair, Namibia supports, in principle, the establishment of a subcommittee on the reduction of greenhouse gas emissions from ships. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Namibia. Please, please. Thank you, Chair, and good morning to all. Our deepest condolences to Kenya and thanks to those providing us with refreshments. Sweden supports, in principle, the establishment of a new subcommittee. We wish to thank the Secretariat for its thorough analysis of the cost implications and other practical considerations, which we have carefully reviewed. We recognize that the substantial work lies ahead. While we are mindful that there are both advantages and disadvantages to organizing this work through a new subcommittee, we have concluded that a new subcommittee would be the most efficient way as it ensures long-term and stable arrangement. Thank you. Thank you, Sweden. China, please. Thank you, Chair. First of all, this delegation would like to convey our sincere condolences to the government and the people of Kenya. We also thank Chile and France for their last days' reception and coffee. This delegation has noted the benefits of establishing a subcommittee. We do not object to considering its establishment. However, we also hope that doing so will not increase the burdens on member states. Therefore, we'd like to suggest based on the Secretary's document, further analyses and the consideration should be given to reforming the existing committees and subcommittees. And to setting appropriate meeting days and frequencies. Our ultimate goal is to address the GHG work related issues without imposing additional burdens on member states. In addition, we also consider that comprehensive consideration should also be given to the delegation between policy and technical discussions in future committees and subcommittees. Given that the main purpose of this extraordinary session is to consider the amendments, we'd like to propose that at a future formal committee meetings, this issue be further considered in depth. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, China. Italy, please. Thank you, Chair. A good morning to all. We would like to express our sincere condolences to Kenya on the passing of the former Prime Minister. And I would appreciate you to the distinguished delegation that can't be offered refreshment yesterday. We also wish to align ourselves with the State and the Liberated by Denmark and Finland. We go glancing the concern as spread by several delegation as well as additional costs of the line in document MAPC, Stroke AC 2, 3 and 4. Submit by Secretary General. We could, in principle, support the proposal to establish a new subcommittee on the reduction of GHG emission from ships under the MAPC AS 2, Stroke 3, Stroke 2. Should this contribute to accelerating and optimising ongoing and future work under the new zero framework? In this context Italy also support the proposal made by UNID Kingdom regarding the definition of thermal reference for the new subcommittee and standard-ready to contribute to the further development. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Italy. Malta, please. Good morning. We are deeply saddened to learn about the passing of Rae Laudinga for the Prime Minister of Kenya. Heartfelt condolences to our Kenya friends and colleagues during this time of the morning. Chair, multiple would like to reaffirm its support for the establishment of a subcommittee on the reduction of GHG emissions from ships under the MAPC. This while acknowledging the financial implications and the need to ensure that the costs remain manageable for both the organisation and its members. Thank you. Thank you, Malta. Spin, please. Thank you, Mr President. Good morning to all the delegations. Our sincere condolences to the delegation of the extent of deepest condolences to the delegation of Kenya. And to pass our thanks to the co-sponsors of the document and we support the establishment of a subcommittee on reduction of GHG emissions from ships. And we support that with a view to facilitating the work being done on the net-serial framework. We are also grateful for the information sent by the Secretary-General on the costs and implications of the proposal. We agree with the secretaries as to the most relevant point at which to have a first meeting of the subcommittee and perhaps in July of 2026. We also take note of the budgetary implications and staffing requirements which the secretaries provided. That's something that has to be taken into account when it comes to planning the budget for the next. Thank you. Thank you, Ben. Eja, please. Thank you, Chair. Allow me as out to express our condolences to Kenya and extend our thanks to all those who hosted us in refreshments throughout the meetings and indeed France for you today's reception. Egypt as a co-sponsor of documents, three slash two fully supports the proposal of the establishment of the subcommittee which in our view became an necessity. We want to repeat the same elements in the presentation of the delegation of Morocco which we fully concur with. However, we would like to comment on some of the points raised during the discussions. It's very important to note that the proposal of delaying the discussion of this issue to a future session means delaying the whole establishment of the new subcommittee for a full biennium. Yet the committee have clear instructions from the council to decide on the matter on hand at MAPCES. We would like a sorry, which means if we delay the proposal of the establishment of the new subcommittee to a future session that the first meeting of the new established subcommittee won't be before sometime in 2028. We would like to thank the Secretary General for the for the commenting document, three slash four and the valuable information on the administrative and financial implications. In the view of this delegation, the provided cost is incompatible to the cost of the whole process. So that in a cost-benefit analysis, ensuring the efficiency of our work would be far beyond the presented cost. Saying that we agree as well with the proposal in the document having the first meeting of the new subcommittee by the end of June, beginning of July 2026. Finally, Chair, we would like to further underline that throughout the process there has been always challenges of the tight timelines along with calls of not delaying the timelines in the 2020-23 IMOG-A-G strategy. Something that created some gaps establishing a new subcommittee on G-A-G will provide a more structured debate with clear agenda items, which we find not difficult having the proposed 14 work streams, which will definitely need lengthy discussions and further detailed work. Yet the new subcommittee provides a solution through its flexibility, having work done in parallel throughout the subcommittee planary, working and drafting groups. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Egypt. Masha Islands, please. Thank you very much, Mr Chair. Just one quick question. Can I bank all the time that people I've not used onto my three minutes? Absolutely. It was worth a try. Thank you very much, Mr Chair. Firstly, let the Masha Islands convey its condolences to the Republic of Kenya on the passing of their Prime Minister. And also a big thank you to all those that contribute into the expansion of my waistline this week. So thank you very much. Mr Chair, the Masha Islands would not object to the establishment of a subcommittee if it is the will of the committee. But I believe we should be mindful of what we are trying to achieve, an effective work mechanism over the next few years. There seems to be a bit of a misconception in my mind that a subcommittee will resolve the multilingualism issues, and it will not. The groups that will meet as identified, the working groups and the drafting groups that we have here in the committees as well as subcommittees, will meet in English. We may need to be mindful of the Cook Islands intervention about paying for interpretation for inter-sessional working group GHGs rather than the establishment of a subcommittee. We also align with the Cook Islands that if a subcommittee is established, which we believe it is the will of the committee at the moment, it should be an organ of both MEPC and MSC. The work streams at MSC on this matter are growing. We would object, however, to a subcommittee meeting three times in the Biannium. This is an ineffective method of work, giving documents, deadlines, reporting lines to committees, et cetera. And you would only end up with eight days of effective working for groups. To achieve the goal, we should consider if a subcommittee is extending a subcommittee once per year to eight days, four days of interpretation, eight days of work. We have experience of this of NCSR and CCC to clear the black backlog of work. I know I have overrun now. On the last thing, I think we really need to be mindful of the resource impact on the Secretary Act. This is huge. Let alone the impact on delegates and the costs and flying in and out of London. On this matter, we would align with two-value, on the expansion of the voluntary multi-donor trust fund, and believe that if a subcommittee is to be established, then we need to review that fund. Thank you very much, Mr. Chair. And... Thank you, Mr. Arland, for not using your bank time. Need a last, please. Dear Chair, thanks for giving me the floor. Firstly, we would like to express our sincere condolences to the delegation of Kenya. On the matter at hand, we would like to thank the Subbitters of the Proposal on the Subcommittee. The Netherlands support, in principle, the establishment of the Subcommittee. As long as it does not interfere with our work on the adopting of the IMO Net Zero framework, as Dylan Gelly explained by the delegation of the UK. Thank you. Thank you, the Netherlands. And again, thanks for having me. Thank you very much, Madam. The press meeting will be finished by now when you get your consent. Our next folly will be answered by Mr. Speaker of the Engineering General Assembly. to provide examinations and measures and the net zero framework on the final implications for the organisation that this initiative is a benefit to all it is in our collective interests and provides us a way forward on this matter thank you thank you good good morning chair, good morning everybody first we would like to register our sincere condolences to the people of Kenya Seryon stands with the proposal for the establishment of a new subcommittee on the adoption of greenhouse gases emission from ships this will be better inform the outcome and the way forward on this most important matter on hand thank you thank you Seryon Slammer we are in place thank you Mr Chair, good Mr Chair, I wish to convey our condolences to the people of Kenya for passing former Prime Minister my delegation experience is full support for the Human MPCES 2-3-2-3-2 proposing the establishment of subcommittee on reduction of GHG emission from ships. This is a timely and strategic response to the complex ID of 13 workers stream under the IMO-NET zero framework and the increasing workload on existing arrangements. However, in line with the Cook Island, Argentina, China and Marshall Island, we also believe there is a room for further consideration of this new subcommittee. We also take note with appreciation of the Secretary General's assessment in document MPCES 2-3-4, which can be confirmed that the establishment of the new subcommittee is administratively and financially visible. Thank you, Mr. Scher.
